job_description: MEAL Officer : Association of Voluntary Actions for Society (AVAS) Jobs ENG বাংলা Create Account Sign In For Employer Create Sign In Home Jobs Mybdjobs Job List Association of Voluntary Actions for Society (AVAS) MEAL Officer Application Deadline : 06 Mar 2026 Shortlist Share: Facebook LinkedIn WhatsApp All Requirements Responsibilities Skills & Expertise Salary & Benefits Company Information Summary Vacancy: 01 Age: 25 to 45 years Location: Patuakhali (Galachipa) Salary: Tk. 45000 - 48000 (Monthly) Published: 26 Feb 2026 Application Insights View Requirements Education Experience Additional Requirements Age 25 to 45 years Responsibilities & Context Responsibilities & Context Association of Voluntary Actions for Society (AVAS) is a national non-profit, rights-based development organization working since 1998 to improve the lives of vulnerable and marginalized communities across Bangladesh. AVAS implements programs in health, gender equality, child protection, climate resilience, livelihood development, education and humanitarian response through strong community engagement and partnerships with government and international development agencies. The organization is committed to accountability, safeguarding, inclusion and sustainable community development. Project Summary The RMNCAH Project aims to strengthen equitable access to quality reproductive, maternal, newborn, child, and adolescent health services at community and facility levels. The programme supports health system strengthening, demand generation, referral pathway improvement, safeguarding integration, and community accountability mechanisms in alignment with national health strategies and donor compliance frameworks. The project emphasizes results-based management, data-driven decision-making, gender equality, and accountability to affected populations. Position Summary The MEAL Officer will lead the implementation and continuous strengthening of the Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning (MEAL) system in line with donor contractual requirements and international best practices. The role will ensure high-quality data collection, verification, analysis, and reporting to measure performance against logframe indicators and ensure compliance with donor visibility, safeguarding, and accountability standards. The MEAL Officer will support adaptive management by generating evidence, identifying implementation gaps, and promoting learning across project components. Key Duties and Responsibilities 1. Monitoring & Results Management Develop and operationalize the project MEAL Plan aligned with the approved logframe and Results Framework. Maintain Indicator Performance Tracking Tables (IPTT) and ensure systematic tracking of output, outcome, and impact indicators. Design data collection tools, guidelines, and reporting templates in compliance with donor requirements. Conduct regular field monitoring visits to assess quality, relevance, coverage, and adherence to technical standards.
